text:
{"claims":[{"statement":"The transforming agent in a century egg is its alkaline material, which gradually raises the pH of the egg from approximately 9 to 12 or more.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"The transforming agent in a century egg is its [alkaline](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alkaline \"Alkaline\") material, which gradually raises the [pH](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PH \"PH\") of the egg from approximately 9 to 12 or more.[\\[54\\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eggs_as_food#cite_note-McGee117-54)","entailment":"The quoted sentence explicitly states that the alkaline material raises the egg's pH from about 9 to 12 or higher, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs provide 647 kJ (155 kcal) of energy per 100 g.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"[Energy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Food_energy \"Food energy\") | 647kJ (155kcal)","entailment":"The table entry lists the energy content as 647 kJ (155 kcal) for a 100 g portion, which directly supports the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"energy content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs contain 10.6 g of fat per 100 g.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"**[Fat](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fat \"Fat\")** | 10.6 g","entailment":"The table entry shows a fat amount of 10.6 g for a 100 g serving, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"fat content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs contain 12.6 g of protein per 100 g.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"**[Protein](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Protein_(nutrient) \"Protein (nutrient)\")** | 12.6 g","entailment":"The table entry lists protein as 12.6 g per 100 g, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"protein content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs provide 373 mg of cholesterol per 100 g.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"[Cholesterol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cholesterol \"Cholesterol\") | 373 mg","entailment":"The table entry records cholesterol at 373 mg for a 100 g portion, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"cholesterol content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs contain 75 g of water per 100 g.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Water | 75 g","entailment":"The table entry lists water as 75 g for a 100 g serving, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"water content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Eggs contain 4 % of the daily value for calcium per 100 g.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"[Calcium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calcium_in_biology#Humans \"Calcium in biology\") | 4%
50 mg","entailment":"The table entry shows calcium at 4 % DV (50 mg) for a 100 g portion, directly supporting the claim.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"calcium content","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"}]}
